root.title: Amber Reference Manual
root.summary: Top of the Amber 2024 Reference Manual. Five parts: (I) intro & install, (II) force fields & energy models, (III) system preparation, (IV) running simulations, (V) analysis.

nodes.title: I. Introduction and Installation
nodes.summary: Introduction and installation: what the Amber/AmberTools suite is, how data flows between its programs, and how to build, update and test a distribution (cmake). Background only — no simulation methods here.

nodes.title: 1. Introduction
nodes.summary: Amber is the collective name for a suite of programs that allow users to carry out molecular dynamics simu- lations, particularly on biomolecules.

nodes.summary: Please send suggestions and questions to amber@ambermd.org. You need to be subscribed to post there; to subscribe, go to http://lists.ambermd.org/mailman/listinfo/amber . You can unsubscribe from this mailing list on the same site.

nodes.title: II. Amber force fields
nodes.summary: Amber force fields and energy models: molecular-mechanics force fields for proteins, nucleic acids, carbohydrates, lipids, ions and water; implicit-solvent models (Generalized Born GB/SA, GBNSR6, Poisson-Boltzmann PBSA, 3D-RISM); and quantum methods (sqm semi-empirical, QUICK ab initio, QM/MM). Choose here for questions about force-field/water-model selection, GB/PB/RISM solvation, or QM.

nodes.summary: Amber is designed to work with several simple types of force fields, although it is most commonly used with parametrizations developed by Peter Kollman and his co-workers and academic“descendants”. The traditional parametrization uses fixed partial charges, centered on atoms.

nodes.title: III. System preparation
nodes.summary: System preparation — turning a structure into simulation-ready topology files: cleaning PDBs (pdb4amber), building systems and adding solvent/ions/bonds in LEaP, reading/editing prmtop parameters (parmed), ligand parameterization with antechamber/GAFF, parameter fitting (paramfit, mdgx), metal sites (pyMSMT), RESP charges (py_resp), and crystal setup. Choose here for tleap, solvateBox, disulfide bonds, ligand parameters, frcmod/mol2.

nodes.summary: The ndfes program [847] reads an input file that describes the biased umbrella sampling, performs the varia- tional free energy profile (vFEP) [847–849], multistate Bennett acceptance ratio (MBAR) [613], weighted ther- modynamic perturbation theory (wTP), [850–852], or generalize…

nodes.title: 44.2. ndfes-PrepareAmberData.py
nodes.summary: --stop,-S A float from (0.0,1.0] representing a time as a fraction of the samples. [Default: 1.0] This discard the samples after “stop” from the analysis. This is useful when performing convergence tests on the sampling.

nodes.title: 44.3. ndfes-CheckEquil.py
nodes.summary: -w,--width The histogram bin width. Use this option multiple times for multiple dimensions. If used only used once, then it assumes each dimension uses the same width. If used multiple times, it must be used the same number of times as the --rxn option.

nodes.title: 44.4. ndfes-CombineMetafiles.py
nodes.summary: The available optional arguments are provided below. -d,--disang The disang file defining the restraint energies. -i,--inp INP The input netcdf trajectory (or disang) file(s).

nodes.title: 44.5. ndfes-PrintFES.py
nodes.summary: Prints the free energies defined in a ndfes checkpoint at the histogram bin centers, for those bins occupied by at least 1 sample. Usage: ndfes-PrintFES.py [-m MODEL] [-c] chkpt The output consists of N+5 columns: 1.

nodes.title: 44.6. ndfes-AvgFESs.py
nodes.summary: Prints the free energies defined in a ndfes checkpoint at the histogram bin centers, for those bins occupied by at least 1 sample. Usage: ndfes-PrintFES.py [-m MODEL] [-c] chkpt The output consists of N+5 columns: 1.

nodes.title: 44.7. ndfes-path
nodes.summary: The available optional arguments are provided below. --fes One or more models to average; e.g., chkptfile=0 will read model 0 from the checkpoint file names ’chkptfile’. If the trailing ’=idx’ is missing, then ’=0’ is assumed.

nodes.title: 44.8. ndfes-path-prepguess.py
nodes.summary: Options for altering the predicted force constants --maxfc Maximum allowable force constants for distance restraints. This value is the largest force constant that you feel comfortable simulating.

nodes.title: 44.9. ndfes-path-analyzesims.py
nodes.summary: --min Either a disang defining a proposed stationary point on the free energy surface. The script will check to see if there is a corresponding restart file by replacing the ’.disang’ suffix with a ’.rst7’ suffix.
